source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/08_Cybersecurity/260303 사용목적 수정(CE 반영)/4. A-CFMEA-HNX_Cybersecurity FMEA table(Rev.4).xlsx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity FMEA Report Rev.4 for the Portable X-Ray system models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports cybersecurity risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk control, and verification evidence for IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirements.
keyPoints: The report identifies cybersecurity hazards using STRIDE across assets and IEC 60601-4-5 requirement IDs.

Risk controls include cryptography, code/data/execution integrity, firmware and software updates, authentication, authorization, confidentiality, event detection and logging, and resiliency and recovery.
Verification references include Cybersecurity Requirements Testing, Source Code Static Analysis, and Penetration Test report no. EBB-2025-000021.
Revision history states Rev.4 added physical controls to mitigate threats to User Identification and Authentication.

entities.name: IEC 60601-4-5
entities.evidence: Added threat identification to the IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirements list.

entities.name: KISA Crypto Module
entities.evidence: A14: Open source 2 - KISA Crypto Module

concepts.name: Risk analysis, risk evaluation, and risk control
Bluetooth secure authentication
concepts.evidence: IA‑04: Secure Authentication for Bluetooth Pairing

concepts.name: Physical access controls
concepts.evidence: IA‑03: Physical Access Controls for Hardware Ports

concepts.name: SBOM-based vulnerability scan
concepts.pageTarget: Software Bill of Materials Vulnerability Management
concepts.evidence: SBOM-based vulnerability scan (using CycloneDX format): Create SBOM in CycloneDX format and periodically review whether each component has CVE

concepts.name: MISRA-C:2004 static analysis
concepts.evidence: Static Analysis (MISRA-C:2004): Perform static analysis based on the MISRA-C ruleset on production code before compilation

concepts.name: Cryptographic integrity verification
concepts.evidence: Implement cryptographic integrity verification for calibration values.

evidence.evidenceText: Cybersecurity FMEA Report

evidence.evidenceText: Product Name,:,Portable X-Ray system

evidence.evidenceText: Model Name,:,HnX-P1 & HnX-PB

evidence.evidenceText: Doc No.,:,A-CFMEA-HNX

evidence.evidenceText: Revision No.,:,Rev.4

evidence.evidenceText: Revision date,:,"July 26, 2025"

evidence.claim: Rev.4 added physical controls to mitigate threats to User Identification and Authentication.
evidence.evidenceText: 4,2025.07.20,Added physical controls to mitigate threats to User Identification and Authentication.

evidence.claim: The FMEA includes risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk control, and implementation of risk control measures.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis (Step 1),,,,Risk evaluation (Step 2),,,,Risk control (Step 3),,,,,APPENDX : Implementation of risk control measures

evidence.claim: The table uses STRIDE categories to identify threats.
evidence.evidenceText: Hazard No.,Asset / Requirement ID,STRIDE,Threat

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements testing is mapped to IEC 60601-4-5 requirement tests.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-4-5 Requirement test

evidence.claim: The configuration hazard CRM-01 addresses malicious modification of configuration settings and uses digital signatures and integrity verification.
evidence.evidenceText: CRM-01,A1: Configuration,Tampering,"Malicious modification of configuration settings, resulting in unsafe operational parameters."...Use digital signatures and integrity verification for configuration settings.

evidence.claim: The calibration hazard CRM-02 addresses unauthorized alteration of calibration values and uses cryptographic integrity verification.
evidence.evidenceText: CRM-02,A2: Calibration,Tampering,Unauthorized alteration of calibration values causing improper X‑ray output...Implement cryptographic integrity verification for calibration values.

evidence.claim: Bluetooth spoofing risk is mitigated by device whitelisting and strong pairing protocols.
evidence.evidenceText: CRM-09,A9: Bluetooth,Spoofing,"Impersonation of a trusted Bluetooth device, compromising secure pairing and data integrity."...Enforce Bluetooth device whitelisting and strong pairing protocols.

evidence.claim: Bluetooth data tampering risk is mitigated with end-to-end encryption and authentication.
evidence.evidenceText: CRM-10,A9: Bluetooth,Tampering,Alteration of data transmitted via Bluetooth affecting the integrity of images or configuration data...Use end-to-end encryption and authentication for Bluetooth communication.

evidence.claim: Hardware interface spoofing risk is mitigated by restricting unauthorized hardware access and enforcing strict authentication.
evidence.evidenceText: CRM-14,A12: Hardware Interfaces,Spoofing,Unauthorized peripheral emulation on physical ports leading to illegitimate connections...Restrict unauthorized hardware access and enforce strict authentication.

evidence.claim: Open source software risk management was added in Rev.2.
evidence.evidenceText: 2,2025.06.02,Revisions to add risk management for open source software

evidence.claim: The STMicroelectronics Open-Source Bundle risks include tampering, spoofing, information disclosure, and elevation of privilege.
evidence.evidenceText: CRM-17,A13: Open source 1 –STMicroelectronics Open-Source Bundle,Tampering...CRM-18,A13: Open source 1 –STMicroelectronics Open-Source Bundle,Spoofing...CRM-19,A13: Open source 1 –STMicroelectronics Open-Source Bundle,Info Disclosure...CRM-20,A13: Open source 1 –STMicroelectronics Open-Source Bundle,Elevation

evidence.claim: The KISA Crypto Module risks include tampering, spoofing, denial of service, and information disclosure.
evidence.evidenceText: CRM-21,A14: Open source 2 - KISA Crypto Module,Tempering...CRM-22,A14: Open source 2 - KISA Crypto Module,Spoofing...CRM-23,A14: Open source 2 - KISA Crypto Module,DoS...CRM-24,A14: Open source 2 - KISA Crypto Module,Info Disclosure

evidence.claim: Open source risk controls include SHA-256 checksum integrity verification, MISRA-C:2004 static analysis, and CycloneDX SBOM vulnerability scanning.
evidence.evidenceText: 1. Integrity Verification (Checksum): Perform SHA-256 verification on all update files...2. Static Analysis (MISRA-C:2004)...3. SBOM-based vulnerability scan (using CycloneDX format)

evidence.claim: For user identification and authentication risks, physical controls include user manual warnings, an X-Ray output warning label, trained users, designated locations, and restriction of unauthorized Bluetooth devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The LCD of this product is a non-touch display, so the following physical controls have been implemented...A warning label for X-Ray output is attached...The device is intended for use by trained users only in designated locations...designed to restrict connection of unauthorized devices.

evidence.claim: The device states that no important data such as patient personal information or diagnostic information is stored in the device.
evidence.evidenceText: No important data such as patient personal information or diagnostic information is stored in the device.

evidence.claim: Verification evidence references Cybersecurity Requirements Testing, Source Code Static Analysis, and Penetration Test report no. EBB-2025-000021.
evidence.evidenceText: Cybersecurity Requirements Testing (Report no. EBB-2025-000021)...Source Code Static Analysis (Report no. EBB-2025-000021)...Penetration Test (Report no. EBB-2025-000021)

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly stated in the source text; authoritySignal only says general.
The header revision date is July 26, 2025, while the revision history lists Rev.4 date as 2025.07.20.
The source appears to be a preprocessed spreadsheet export with CSV-like formatting and some typographical/OCR artifacts such as APPENDX, Impack, Tempering, and Very low.
